frontier.hypothesis: F2 (unchanged): tree-inline.c under -O2 preserves the function-body boundary for `static inline` helpers, closing the helper-extraction angle by construction.
frontier.mechanism: GCC 2.7.2 has no LTO; -O2 always_inline decisions run at tree-optimize; if inlining lands before RTL emit with fresh flow.c reg_n_sets on the caller, helper-extraction opens a novel RTL context (F2 OPEN). If the function-body boundary is preserved through cc1, emitted bytes are split across two function labels (F2 CLOSED-BY-CONSTRUCTION).
frontier.next_probe: Static-analysis session: read tools/gcc-2.7.2/tree-inline.c + integrate.c to determine `static inline` handling under -O2; cross-check whether integrate.c::expand_inline_function fires at cc1 time or preserves the CALL_EXPR through to RTL emit. No src edit, no sandbox measurement — closure-by-construction verdict is the deliverable.

frontier.hypothesis: Speculative axis (unchanged): asymmetric ref-lift — a C construct that lifts ONLY arg5's refs (or ONLY lowers t0's refs) without symmetric wrap/dup effect.
frontier.mechanism: local-alloc.c qty_compare uses per-pseudo REG_N_REFS at flow-time. To flip arg5>t0 (arg5 pri needs >=5000 per s1 fable-blitz arithmetic, currently 3333 vs t0's 5000), need to raise arg5 refs to >=4 at span 6, or lower t0 refs below 6 at span 24.
frontier.next_probe: Structural session (novel axis, not permuter): survey RTL emit for whether t0's 6 refs can be reduced via C-source spelling (e.g. compute t0 chain in a DIFFERENT scope so cse folds one ref away without dropping into g3 basin) OR arg5's 2 refs can be doubled via a legitimate additional use. Requires careful cheat-vetting.

frontier.hypothesis: F1-derivative (NEW this session): reload-substitution axis on csmd4 — engineer csmd4's outer control flow so reload substitutes block=3 fresh-dest pseudos into already-multi-set hard-regs, dropping LAUNCH activity to 0 the way saEft01Init does.
frontier.mechanism: Reload pass renumbers pseudos to hard-regs and can leave the block=3 SET dests multi-set at sched2 time. If v0/v1/a0 gain enough fn-scope contention (multi-set writes upstream and downstream of the debug_printf block), reload may substitute p106/p107 into those hard-regs, killing the LAUNCH-tie.
frontier.next_probe: This angle converges with the already-explored fn-scope-refs bank (s5 F1a/F1b/F2a/F2b, s13 fn-body-hoist, s31 5-slot prologue permuter, s32 PERM_GENERAL fresh-seed). To reopen: identify a fn-scope multi-set pattern NOT in the rejected bank (e.g. multi-set writes to D_800F19BC or D_800F19C0 that create hard-reg contention on v0/v1/a0 through a legitimate program logic path, not a coercion). High cheat-vetting risk.
